items.title: Five whale entanglements in five days on Ningaloo Reef

children.content: The decomposing carcass of a humpback whale closes two popular West Australian beaches as sharks converge on the area — but experts say the cause of the whale's death is of greater concern.

gallery.altText: Sharks swarm the pale carcass of a humpback whale.

thumbnail.altText: Sharks swarm the pale carcass of a humpback whale.

items.title: How a young doctor ended up being charged with manslaughter after street scuffle

children.content: A young Perth doctor and musician is behind bars, accused of killing a 63-year-old man after a late-night Fremantle scuffle. What do we know?

image.altText: Mr Joseph wearing a graduating gown and holding frame

items.title: 'Ovulating, come over now': The seedy side of online sperm donation

children.content: Australia is facing a sperm donation shortage and it is leading those desperate to find a way to conceive down some expensive and potentially dangerous paths.

items.title: Blind bowler's dream threatened by NDIS funding cuts

children.content: A blind bowler from regional WA say his dreams of representing Australia are being swept away along with his NDIS funding.

gallery.altText: A middle-aged man in a cap and sports tracksuit top stands in front of a bowling green.

items.title: 'I have everything to ruin your life': Sextortion threats on the rise

children.content: Major online platforms are accused of failing to deploy readily available technology to detect well-known blackmail tactics.

image.altText: Girl at computer with emojis and speech bubble reading 'Are you alone?'

items.title: Angus Taylor says he criticised One Nation with a 'heavy heart'

children.content: The Opposition Leader says he reluctantly made a speech criticising Pauline Hanson's One Nation party policies. Follow live.

items.title: Oil posts biggest gain since 2020 as Middle East war fears reignite

children.content: Oil prices soar more than 9 per cent as the US president threatens to blockade the Strait of Hormuz. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A 3D printed oil pump jack is placed on dollar banknotes in this illustration picture

items.title: Violence against women under national spotlight after four deaths in four days

children.content: Experts are calling for more research and better funding for domestic violence prevention after four women and girls were killed in separate incidents last week.

image.altText: A composite showing photos of three women, and a fourth picture that shows the shadow outline of a girl

items.title: 'So many questions remain unanswered': 25 years since the Peter Falconio case

children.content: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend Joanne Lees in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.

gallery.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.
A man in a pale shirt is held by a serious-looking police officer.
Old picture of Peter Falconio (left) at his graduation with girlfriend Joanne Lees (right)

thumbnail.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.
A man in a pale shirt is held by a serious-looking police officer.
Old picture of Peter Falconio (left) at his graduation with girlfriend Joanne Lees (right)

items.title: Mixed martial arts group attempts to gift $82k Cognac to Brisbane Olympic committee

children.content: The "schmoozing", revealed exclusively by the ABC today, is part of a wider series of examples of attempted influence-peddling from businesses and sporting bodies before the Games kick off in 2032.

image.altText: a bottle of cognac next to two glasses on top of a barrell

items.title: Man who sneaked into Australia 45 years ago has lived in limbo until now

children.content: Kwong Wan Lo stowed away on a ship bound for Australia 45 years ago. With no legal right to stay, he built a life in Sydney anyway.

image.altText: A portrait of Kwong Wan Lo wearing a black jacket.

items.title: Golfers tee off for first aid to help save lives in WA's Wheatbelt

children.content: The long wait for an ambulance in WA's Wheatbelt makes first aid critical, and these golfers are ensuring their community is well prepared.

image.altText: Four people with golf clubs stand next to a hole

items.title: Jewish professor says 'intimidating' protest changed his life on campus

children.content: Melbourne University professor Steven Prawer hopes the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will lead to campus change, following a 2024 demonstration that saw protesters invade his office.

image.altText: Man in blue shirt stands in front of office window and indoor plant

items.title: Family of Perth man raises questions over his Bali death

children.content: Cameron Hughes had been living in Bali for more than 15 years, and his family says he had been trying to extend his visa to stay close to his son before he died in an immigration detention centre.

children.content: The process was simple and the service was speedy but the ease with which 7.30 acquired illicit tobacco products was concerning.

items.title: Premier Roger Cook takes over defence industries in cabinet reshuffle

items.title: Richard Scolyer encouraged Australians to enrol in research. Here's how

items.title: De Minaur ends 17-year Australian drought with career-high ranking

children.content: Alex de Minaur's arrow keeps pointing upwards despite his wait for a grand slam title being lengthened by a disappointing Wimbledon exit.

items.title: Numbat population improves amid warning there is a long way to go

children.content: Numbats, Western Australia's animal emblem, have been pulled from the brink of extinction after research found the population was growing in the wild.

items.title: How India uranium deal could unlock billions for resources powerhouse of WA

children.content: Australia has struck a uranium exports deal with India, which has put WA’s effective ban on uranium mining and exporting in the spotlight.
